cities.name: Alanya
cities.tagline: Antalya's bigger, cheaper, beach-first coastal cousin — and home to Cleopatra Beach.
cities.summary: Alanya stretches 25 km along the Mediterranean east of Antalya. Cleopatra Beach / Center has the castle views and the most walkable base. Oba and Tosmur are mid-range residential. Mahmutlar is the expat-heavy value zone. Pick based on how much resort vs. local-life you want.

areas.name: Alanya Center & Cleopatra Beach
areas.oneLiner: The postcard side — castle on the cliff, sandy Cleopatra Beach, busy promenade.
pros: Best sandy beach in the region

areas.name: Oba & Tosmur
areas.oneLiner: The quieter residential strip just east of the center — mid-range resorts, less chaos.
pros: Dolmuş to the center in 10 min

areas.name: Mahmutlar
areas.oneLiner: The expat value zone — long pebble beach, Russian/German shopfronts, cheap long-term stays.
pros: Cheapest hotels and rentals in the region
cons: Feels quite Eastern European in summer

areas.name: Avsallar & Okurcalar
areas.oneLiner: 30 min west of Alanya — all-inclusive resort strip with long sandy beaches.

hotels.name: Delphin Be Grand Resort
hotels.whyStay: One of the best all-inclusives on the strip — excellent food, large pool complex, private beach.

hotels.name: Granada Luxury Beach
hotels.whyStay: Large family-friendly 5-star with waterpark, kids' programs, beachfront setting.

hotels.name: Hedef Resort & Spa
hotels.whyStay: Cliffside hotel with elevator down to a private beach — great value 5-star near the center.

hotels.name: Club Hotel Sera
hotels.whyStay: Large resort near Cleopatra Beach with good all-inclusive food and multiple pools.

hotels.name: Grand Kaptan Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Beachfront family resort with waterslides, kids' club, and reasonable all-inclusive pricing.

hotels.name: Xperia Saray Beach
hotels.whyStay: Reliable mid-range family hotel with a pool, 5 min from the beach.

hotels.name: White City Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Modern apart-hotel with kitchenettes near Mahmutlar beach — popular with digital nomads.

hotels.name: Orion Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Good-value central Mahmutlar hotel with a rooftop pool and short walk to the beach.

faqs.a: Alanya is cheaper, beachier, less cultural. Antalya has more history (Kaleiçi), better old town, and easier international flights. Many travelers do 2 nights Antalya + 5 nights Alanya.
Yes — it's the only long sandy beach in the area and has water the Roman queen supposedly bathed in. Get there before 10am for a good spot in summer.

cities.heroImageCredit: Red Tower & harbour, Alanya / Wikimedia Commons

cities.name: Side
cities.tagline: Roman ruins on the beach — a resort town built inside a 2,000-year-old city.
cities.summary: Side is small and unusual — a former Greek-Roman port where you walk past ancient temples on the way to dinner. The Old Town peninsula is pedestrianized and romantic. West Side and East Side have the large all-inclusive resorts.

areas.name: Side Old Town
areas.oneLiner: The pedestrianized Roman peninsula — temple columns at sunset, restaurants everywhere, harbor walks.

areas.name: West Side (Kumköy)
areas.oneLiner: The long sandy beach strip west of Old Town — large 5-star all-inclusives.
pros: Walkable to Old Town in ~25 min or free shuttle

areas.name: East Side (Sorgun)
areas.oneLiner: Forested beach area east of the peninsula — quieter, upscale resorts.
cons: Further from the Old Town walk

hotels.name: Barut Hemera
hotels.whyStay: Adults-only Barut resort with excellent food, big pools, direct beach access — top-tier Turkish all-inclusive.

hotels.name: Sueno Hotels Beach Side
hotels.whyStay: Huge family-friendly resort with waterpark, multiple restaurants, and long beach.

hotels.name: Ali Bey Resort
hotels.whyStay: Legendary family all-inclusive with tennis academy and vast kids' programs.

hotels.name: Crystal Sunset Luxury Resort
hotels.whyStay: Well-reviewed family resort with a long private beach and excellent buffet.

hotels.name: Side Crown Charm Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Boutique hotel steps from the temple of Apollo in Old Town — great for a cultural base.

hotels.name: Beach House Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Long-running adults-only hotel in the heart of Old Town with a seafront terrace.

faqs.q: Should I stay in Side Old Town or a resort?
faqs.a: Old Town if you want to walk to dinner among Roman ruins. A resort if you want all-inclusive convenience. A smart combo: 2 nights boutique in Old Town, 4 nights in an East Side resort.

cities.name: Kuşadası
cities.tagline: Cruise-ship gateway to Ephesus — and Turkey's most walkable Aegean resort town.
cities.summary: Kuşadası is the main port of call for Aegean cruises and the base most travelers pick for visiting Ephesus. Stay in Kuşadası Center for walking access to restaurants and the marina. Ladies Beach is the classic beach base. Selçuk is the tiny town right next to Ephesus itself.

areas.name: Kuşadası Center
areas.oneLiner: Walking distance to the port, the grand bazaar, and a strip of harbor-view restaurants.
pros: Best dining variety in the area

areas.name: Ladies Beach (Kadınlar Denizi)
areas.oneLiner: The main Kuşadası beach — hotel row along the bay, family-friendly, with dolmuş to the center.
cons: Less walkable to restaurants than the center

areas.name: Selçuk (for Ephesus)
areas.oneLiner: Tiny town 5 min from the Ephesus ruins — the best base for dawn visits before the buses arrive.
pros: Great for early or sunset visits
cons: Not on the sea — no beach

hotels.name: Korumar Ephesus Beach & Spa
hotels.whyStay: Top-rated 5-star on the coast with adults-only areas, excellent spa, and panoramic sea views.

hotels.name: Charisma De Luxe Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Adults-only cliffside 5-star with private beach platforms and great dining.

hotels.name: Sealight Resort Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Large family resort with multiple pools and good all-inclusive value.

hotels.name: Richmond Ephesus Resort
hotels.whyStay: Beachside 5-star with a big pool and very strong family reputation.

hotels.name: Grand Efe Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Nicest mid-range in Selçuk, 5-min drive to Ephesus, pool and garden.

hotels.name: Nilya Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Restored Ottoman house in central Selçuk with garden courtyard, family-run, excellent reviews.

hotels.name: Villa Konak
hotels.whyStay: Charming stone guesthouse in Selçuk old quarter, peaceful courtyard, great value.

faqs.q: Should I stay in Kuşadası or Selçuk for Ephesus?
faqs.a: Selçuk if Ephesus is your only goal — you can be inside the ruins at dawn before the crowds. Kuşadası if you want a broader vacation with beach, dining, and a cruise option.

faqs.q: How far is Ephesus from Kuşadası?
faqs.a: Around 20 minutes by car. Day tours pick up at Kuşadası hotels every morning.

cities.name: Mersin
cities.tagline: The eastern Mediterranean city — cleaner beaches, lower prices, no tour buses.
cities.summary: Mersin is a modern Turkish port city few foreign tourists reach — which is exactly the point. Stay in Yenişehir for the walkable seafront and restaurants. Mezitli has the beach hotels. Erdemli & Kızkalesi (60 min east) have the famous sea castles and quieter coves.

areas.name: Yenişehir
areas.oneLiner: Mersin's modern heart — long seafront promenade, shopping, and the best restaurants.
pros: Best restaurant scene in the city

areas.name: Mezitli & Tece
areas.oneLiner: Mersin's beach suburb — row of beach hotels, open-air cafés, local Sunday market.

areas.name: Kızkalesi (Erdemli)
areas.oneLiner: An hour east of Mersin — the famous Maiden's Castle sits in the sea just off a sandy beach.
pros: One of Turkey's most photogenic castles

hotels.name: Mersin HiltonSA
hotels.whyStay: The landmark 5-star on Mersin's seafront promenade — big pool, sea views, central Yenişehir location.

hotels.name: Divan Mersin
hotels.whyStay: Modern 5-star with rooftop restaurant (Roof 14), walkable to the marina, 9.3 location rating.

hotels.name: The Qua Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Well-reviewed modern hotel near the Yenişehir Forum mall. Good value for a city stay.

hotels.name: Vista Boutique Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Relaxed beach-adjacent hotel in Mezitli with private balconies — great value near Viransehir Beach.

hotels.name: Merada Suit Otel and Spa
hotels.whyStay: Suite-style rooms with kitchenettes in central Mezitli, garden terrace — good for longer stays.

hotels.name: Kilikya Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Beachfront 4-star directly opposite the Maiden's Castle — private beach, pool, kids' water park.

hotels.name: Yaka Hotel Kizkalesi
hotels.whyStay: Simple sea-view hotel in Kızkalesi with direct castle views — incredible value for the area.

hotels.name: Sahil Martı Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Beachfront 4-star in Mezitli with private sand area, Turkish bath and sea-view rooms — the easiest swim-out-of-bed option in Mersin.

hotels.name: Rain Hotel Mersin
hotels.whyStay: Clean modern budget hotel in central Yenişehir, walkable to the seafront promenade — strong reviews for the price.

faqs.q: Is Mersin worth visiting as a tourist?
faqs.a: Yes — if you want a Turkey that isn't on every Instagram feed. Mersin is modern, safe, and priced for locals. Pair it with Kızkalesi or the ruins at Kanlıdivane for a different side of the country.
Very safe in the city itself — it's a major Turkish port with a large middle class. Standard urban caution applies; avoid the port industrial areas after dark.
